name: Revive Teammate
description: A player restores a downed teammate through a channel, interaction, item, or protected rescue sequence.
design_notes: Revive mechanics are cooperative transactions with high grief risk. The design needs revive_target_id, reviver_player_id, channel progress, interruption rules, invulnerability after recovery, and authority over the downed state so clients cannot spoof rescues or chain-revive forever.
design_purpose: Keep co-op players engaged after failure by giving teammates a recovery objective.
Create tactical decisions around risk, positioning, cover, and resource use.
Reward teamwork without erasing the consequences of taking lethal damage.
player_fantasy: The player feels heroic when they reach a teammate under pressure and bring them back into the fight.

parameters.name: revive_target_id
parameters.description: Player or character identifier for the teammate currently eligible for revival.

parameters.name: reviver_player_id
parameters.description: Player attempting the revive interaction and receiving channel ownership.

parameters.name: revive_channel_ms
parameters.description: Required uninterrupted channel window before the authority commits recovery.

parameters.name: interruption_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule set that cancels or preserves revive progress under damage, movement, or target changes.

parameters.name: post_revive_invulnerability_ms
parameters.description: Short protection window after recovery to prevent instant re-downing.

parameters.name: chain_revive_lock_ms
parameters.description: Lockout that prevents repeated revive loops or exploitative downed-state cycling.

edge_cases: Two teammates revive the same target and one cancels while the other should continue.
The downed player disconnects, bleeds out, or changes teams during the revive channel.
The reviver loses line of sight or moves out of interaction radius due to knockback or platform motion.
A revive completes on the client but the server rejects it because damage interrupted the channel.
common_bugs: Revive progress is owned by the client and can be completed after the target is no longer downed.
Animation starts on both players at different ticks and the target snaps or stands early.
Post-revive invulnerability applies to all damage sources, including hazards that should remain dangerous.
Chain revive lockout is not saved and resets after reconnect or host migration.
balancing_notes: Long channels create rescue tension, but they require enough cover, crowd control, or revive tools to feel fair.
Invulnerability should prevent spawn-kill frustration without letting revived players safely attack for free.
Limited revive resources can heighten stakes, but depleted players still need meaningful spectate or support options.
accessibility_notes: Offer hold, toggle, or repeated-press revive input options for players with different motor needs.
Show downed timers, interruption causes, and revive progress through text and icons, not only radial fills.

implementation_notes.unity: Use a server-owned revive interaction record with interaction_request_id, reviver_player_id, revive_target_id, start tick, and interruption_rule_id. Clients can animate predicted progress, but Netcode should commit health, downed state, invulnerability, and lockout from one authority event.
implementation_notes.godot: Represent revive as an authoritative interaction state keyed by target and reviver. RPCs request start and cancel; the server verifies distance, team, downed state, and interruptions before broadcasting synchronized animation and recovery snapshots.
implementation_notes.unreal: GameMode or a server component should own downed and revive state. Use server RPCs for start/cancel, replicated progress for UI, Gameplay Tags for interruption and invulnerability, and a single commit path for health restoration.
implementation_notes.web: For WebSocket co-op, send revive requests with interaction_request_id and client_input_sequence, then let the server advance channel progress by server_tick. Browser clients should reconcile predicted progress when interruption or target state changes.

example_games.title: Left 4 Dead
example_games.note: Uses teammate rescue and recovery as a core cooperative pressure point.

example_games.title: Apex Legends
example_games.note: Uses downed states, revives, and respawn recovery within squad play.
